items.title: Iroko team with sights set firmly on Grand National glory
items.summary: Trainers Oliver Greenall and Josh Guerriero believe Grand National favourite Iroko will be all the better for his experience from the race last year.

items.title: Voormalig Ajacied Rugani vindt na 'zware periode' nieuwe club in Italië
items.summary: Daniele Rugani heeft tijdelijk een nieuwe club gevonden. De voormalig verdediger van Ajax is voor een half seizoen van Juventus verhuurd aan Fiorentina, al had hij naar eigen zeggen ook andere opties. 'Ik heb een zware tijd achter de rug.'

items.title: Tudor says 'no time for excuses' about Spurs form
items.summary: New Tottenham Hotspur interim manager Igor Tudor says "nobody can accept" the club's current position.

items.title: Igor Tudor hopes courage and confidence can arrest Tottenham slide
items.summary: Interim manager: ‘There is no time to find excuses’ ‘The position of the club is one that nobody can accept’ Igor Tudor says there is “no time to find excuses” as he attempts to halt Tottenham’s Premier League slide. The 47-year-old former Juventus manager, brought in last week as the sacked Thomas Frank’s replacement until the end of the season, believes instilling the players with confidence is his most important task. “The team need, I believe, first of all, to get some confidence, to get some courage, but also, in same way, the concrete things in the pitch,” Tudor told Spursplay. “Of course, I’m coming here knowing that situation is not easy. There is no time to find excuses. What I said from the first day here, each of us need to give something something more, something extra.” Continue reading...

items.title: Vítor Pereira insists he shares trust with ‘emotional’ Evangelos Marinakis
items.summary: ‘He trusts my work, I trust his personality’ Coach and owner have worked together at Olympiakos Vítor Pereira believes the trust he has with Nottingham Forest’s owner, Evangelos Marinakis, means he can survive the poisoned chalice of the City Ground job. The Portuguese has become Forest’s fourth manager of the season after Sean Dyche followed Nuno Espírito Santo and Ange Postecoglou out of the door as Marinakis acted in an attempt to avoid Premier League relegation. Continue reading...

items.title: De jongste doelpuntenmaker van Jong Ajax ooit: 'Ik was wel écht blij, hoor'
items.summary: Voor Mohamed Abdalla werd de wedstrijd van Jong Ajax tegen SC Cambuur er één om niet snel te vergeten. De zestienjarige middenvelder nam de 1-1 voor zijn rekening en werd daarmee de jongste doelpuntenmaker ooit voor het Amsterdamse beloftenelftal in de Keuken Kampioen Divisie.

items.title: Arteta suggests warmup change amid injuries
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has revealed Arsenal are assessing their warm-up routines after four players suffered fitness scares just before matches.

items.title: Luis Enrique slams 'worthless' Dembélé PSG take
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain coach Luis Enrique said he will not allow any player to be bigger than the club ahead of Tuesday's Champions League knockout phase playoff first leg against AS Monaco.

items.title: Klopp's agent: Man Utd, Chelsea made approach
items.summary: Jurgen Klopp's agent Marc Kosicke said his client had his pick of teams to manage after leaving Liverpool, including approaches from both the United States and England to take over as coach of their respective national teams.

items.title: De ongelooflijke reis van de Rotterdammer die plots bij Benfica schittert
items.summary: Sidny Lopes Cabral (23) werd onlangs naast Rui Costa gepresenteerd als nieuwe aanwinst van Benfica. Niet veel later liet hij onder toeziend oog van José Mourinho het Estádio da Luz ontploffen. Heel lang klonken dergelijke hoogtepunten slechts als een utopie voor de vleugelspeler. Drie jaar geleden speelde hij namelijk op het vijfde niveau van Duitsland en ruim een half jaar terug nog in de 3. Liga. Nu wachten de Champions League én het WK. ‘Mijn carrière is een crazy verhaal.’

items.title: Henderson: Mind changing every day on Constitution Hill's Cheltenham dilemma
items.summary: Nicky Henderson admits he is yet to decide whether to run Constitution Hill in the 2026 Champion Hurdle after his star's recent falls.

items.title: AZ in actie: het programma in tussenronde Europa en Conference League
items.summary: Deze donderdag wordt naast de Europa League ook de Conference League hervat met de tussenronde. In tegenstelling tot de Champions League en Europa League is er nog wél Nederlandse inbreng, want AZ is nog altijd actief in het toernooi. De Alkmaarders nemen het in de tussenronde op tegen FC Noah .

items.title: Pollock to make first England start against Ireland as Itoje set for 100th cap
items.summary: Henry Pollock will make his first Test start in Saturday's Six Nations clash against Ireland, with captain Maro Itoje poised to earn his 100th England cap.

items.title: Arsenal make Saka best-paid player with new five-year deal
items.summary: England winger Bukayo Saka commits his future to Premier League leaders Arsenal by signing a new five-year deal.

items.title: Ook zonder Schreuder blijft CD Castellón records breken
items.summary: Onder trainer Dick Schreuder klom CD Castellón in Spanje naar de Segunda División en ook na het vertrek van de Nederlandse coach blijft de club het goed doen. Waar Schreuder dit seizoen hoge ogen gooit met NEC, is Castellón koploper op het tweede niveau van Spanje en werd een clubrecord neergezet.

items.title: Cricket legends urge 'humane and dignified' treatment for Khan in Pakistan jail
items.summary: Fourteen former international cricket captains including ex-England skippers Nasser Hussain and Mike Atherton have issued a humanitarian appeal for Imran Khan amid concern over his health in jail.

items.title: 'We created fire' - Forest boss Pereira on Marinakis reunion
items.summary: Nottingham Forest boss Vitor Pereira says "he trusts my work, I trust his personality" as he is reunited with Evangelos Marinakis.

items.title: ‘We had fun times’: Dennis Wise on the Crazy Gang, Chelsea and Como
items.summary: Wise remembers long throws with Vinnie Jones, training in a park with Gus Poyet and scoring in Europe for Millwall By The Coaches’ Voice As a young player I had been told a few times that I wasn’t quite good enough. Wimbledon manager Dave Bassett was the one who looked at me in a different way. He was the man who gave me that all-important opportunity. In terms of structuring a team, he was on the ball in everything he did. He was a long way in front of a lot of others, but because of the way he was, people looked at him in a different way. If he had been well-spoken and had what you might call an intelligent way about him, people would have looked at him differently. They would have said: “Wow, this guy is miles ahead.” Continue reading...

items.title: Premier League boosted by 25% rise in South American rights with £450m ESPN deal
items.summary: South American and Caribbean partnership runs to 2031 Substantial rise contrasts with stagnant domestic rights The Premier League has secured a 25% increase on the value of its broadcast contact in South America and the Caribbean by agreeing a new deal with the American network ESPN. The Guardian has learned that ESPN has agreed to extend its longstanding partnership until 2031, with the new deal understood to be worth about £450m. The Premier League told the clubs about the new deal at the shareholders’ meeting last Friday, when they were also updated about TV rights contracts in other regions that are due to go out to tender shortly. Continue reading...

items.title: From Arsenal to FK Qarabag: Why every team left will, won't win the Champions League
items.summary: For each of the 24 remaining teams with a shot at the Champions League crown, let's talk about both why they could win the whole thing ... and why they probably won't.
